{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "200:                 unique case (debug_addr_i[6:2])
201:                   5'b0_0000: jump_req_n = 1'b1; // DNPC
202:                   default:;
203:                 endcase
204:               end
205:             end
206: 
207:             6'b00_0100: begin // General-Purpose Registers
208:               debug_gnt_o = 1'b1; // grant it even when invalid access to not block
209: 
210:               if (debug_halted_o) begin
211:                 regfile_wreq = 1'b1;
212:               end
213:             end
214: 
215:             default: debug_gnt_o = 1'b1; // grant it even when invalid access to not block
216:           endcase
217:         end
218:       end else begin
219:         //----------------------------------------------------------------------------
220:         // read access
221:         //----------------------------------------------------------------------------
222:         if (debug_addr_i[14]) begin
223:           debug_gnt_o = 1'b1; // grant it even when invalid access to not block
224: 
225:           // CSR access
226:           if (debug_halted_o) begin
227:             // access to internal registers are only allowed when the core is halted
228:             csr_req_n   = 1'b1;
229:             rdata_sel_n = RD_CSR;
230:           end
231:         end else begin
232:           // non-CSR access
233:           unique case (debug_addr_i[13:8])
234:             6'b00_0000: begin // Debug Registers, always accessible
235:               debug_gnt_o = 1'b1;
236: 
237:               rdata_sel_n = RD_DBGA;
238:             end
239: 
240:             6'b10_0000: begin // Debug Registers, only accessible when in debug
241:               debug_gnt_o = 1'b1; // grant it even when invalid access to not block
242: 
243:               rdata_sel_n = RD_DBGS;
244:             end
245: 
246:             6'b00_0100: begin // General-Purpose Registers
247:               debug_gnt_o = 1'b1; // grant it even when invalid access to not block
248: 
249:               if (debug_halted_o) begin
250:                 regfile_rreq_n = 1'b1;
251:                 rdata_sel_n    = RD_GPR;
252:               end
253:             end
254: 
255:             default: debug_gnt_o = 1'b1; // grant it even when invalid access to not block
256:           endcase
257:         end
258:       end
259:     end
260:   end
261: 
262:   //----------------------------------------------------------------------------
263:   // debug register read access
264:   //
265:   // Since those are combinational, we can do it in the cycle where we set
266:   // rvalid. The address has been latched into addr_q
267:   //----------------------------------------------------------------------------
268:   always_comb
269:   begin
270:     dbg_rdata = '0;
271: 
272:     case (rdata_sel_q)
273:       RD_DBGA: begin
274:         unique case (addr_q[6:2])
275:           5'h00: dbg_rdata[31:0] = {15'b0, debug_halted_o, 15'b0, settings_q[DBG_SETS_SSTE]}; // DBG_CTRL
276:           5'h01: dbg_rdata[31:0] = {15'b0, sleeping_i, 15'b0, dbg_ssth_q}; // DBG_HIT
277:           5'h02: begin // DBG_IE
278:             dbg_rdata[31:16] = '0;
279:             dbg_rdata[15:12] = '0;
280:             dbg_rdata[11]    = settings_q[DBG_SETS_ECALL];
281:             dbg_rdata[10: 8] = '0;
282:             dbg_rdata[ 7]    = settings_q[DBG_SETS_ELSU];
283:             dbg_rdata[ 6]    = 1'b0;
284:             dbg_rdata[ 5]    = settings_q[DBG_SETS_ELSU];
285:             dbg_rdata[ 4]    = 1'b0;
286:             dbg_rdata[ 3]    = settings_q[DBG_SETS_EBRK];
287:             dbg_rdata[ 2]    = settings_q[DBG_SETS_EILL];
288:             dbg_rdata[ 1: 0] = '0;
289:           end
290:           5'h03: dbg_rdata = {dbg_cause_q[5], 26'b0, dbg_cause_q[4:0]}; // DBG_CAUSE
291:           5'h10: dbg_rdata = '0; // DBG_BPCTRL0
292:           5'h12: dbg_rda
[TRUNCATED]

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "195:     case (csr_addr_i)
196:       // mstatus: IE bit
197:       12'h300: if (csr_we_int) begin
198:           mstatus_n = '{
199:           mie:  csr_wdata_int[`MSTATUS_MIE_BITS],
200:           mpie: csr_wdata_int[`MSTATUS_MPIE_BITS],
201:           mpp:  PrivLvl_t'(PRIV_LVL_M)
202:         };
203:       end
204:       // mepc: exception program counter
205:       12'h341: if (csr_we_int) mepc_n = csr_wdata_int;
206:       // mcause
207:       12'h342: if (csr_we_int) mcause_n = {csr_wdata_int[31], csr_wdata_int[4:0]};
208: 		default: ;
209:     endcase
210: 
211:     // exception controller gets priority over other writes
212:     unique case (1'b1)
213: 
214:       csr_save_cause_i: begin
215:         unique case (1'b1)
216:           csr_save_if_i:
217:             mepc_n = pc_if_i;
218:           csr_save_id_i:
219:             mepc_n = pc_id_i;
220:           default:;
221:         endcase
222:         mstatus_n.mpie = mstatus_q.mie;
223:         mstatus_n.mie  = 1'b0;
224:         mcause_n       = csr_cause_i;
225:       end //csr_save_cause_i
226: 
227:       csr_restore_mret_i: begin //MRET
228:         mstatus_n.mie  = mstatus_q.mpie;
229:         mstatus_n.mpie = 1'b1;
230:       end //csr_restore_mret_i
231: 
232:       default:;
233: 
234:     endcase
235: 
236:   end
237: 
238: 
239:   // CSR operation logic
240:   always_comb
241:   begin
242:     csr_wdata_int = csr_wdata_i;
243:     csr_we_int    = 1'b1;
244: 
245:     unique case (csr_op_i)
246:       CSR_OP_WRITE: csr_wdata_int = csr_wdata_i;
247:       CSR_OP_SET:   csr_wdata_int = csr_wdata_i | csr_rdata_o;
248:       CSR_OP_CLEAR: csr_wdata_int = (~csr_wdata_i) & csr_rdata_o;
249: 
250:       CSR_OP_NONE: begin
251:         csr_wdata_int = csr_wdata_i;
252:         csr_we_int    = 1'b0;
253:       end
254: 
255:       default:;
256:     endcase
257:   end
258: 
259: 
260:   // output mux
261:   always_comb
262:   begin
263:     csr_rdata_o = csr_rdata_int;
264: 
265:     // performance counters
266:     if (is_pccr || is_pcer || is_pcmr)
267:       csr_rdata_o = perf_rdata;
268:   end
269: 
270: 
271:   // directly output some registers
272:   assign m_irq_enable_o  = mstatus_q.mie;
273:   assign mepc_o          = mepc_q;
274: 
275: 
276:   // actual registers
277:   always_ff @(posedge clk, negedge rst_n)
278:   begin
279:     if (rst_n == 1'b0)
280:     begin
281:       mstatus_q  <= '{
282:               mie:  1'b0,
283:               mpie: 1'b0,
284:               mpp:  PRIV_LVL_M
285:             };
286:       mepc_q     <= '0;
287:       mcause_q   <= '0;
288:     end
289:     else
290:     begin
291:       // update CSRs
292:         mstatus_q  <= '{
293:                 mie:  mstatus_n.mie,
294:                 mpie: mstatus_n.mpie,
295:                 mpp:  PRIV_LVL_M
296:               };
297:       mepc_q     <= mepc_n;
298:       mcause_q   <= mcause_n;
299:     end
300:   end
301: 
302:   /////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
303:   //   ____            __     ____                  _            //
304:   // |  _ \ ___ _ __ / _|   / ___|___  _   _ _ __ | |_ ___ _ __  //
305:   // | |_) / _ \ '__| |_   | |   / _ \| | | | '_ \| __/ _ \ '__| //
306:   // |  __/  __/ |  |  _|  | |__| (_) | |_| | | | | ||  __/ |    //
307:   // |_|   \___|_|  |_|(_)  \____\___/ \__,_|_| |_|\__\___|_|    //
308:   //                                                             //
309:   /////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
310: 
311:   assign PCCR_in[0]  = 1'b1;                          // cycle counter
312:   assign PCCR_in[1]  = if_valid_i;                    // instruction counter
313:   assign PCCR_in[2]  = 1'b0;                          // Reserved
314:   assign PCCR_in[3]  = 1'b0;                          // Reserved
315
[TRUNCATED]

340:       end
341: 
342:       OPCODE_OPIMM: begin // Register-Immediate ALU Operations
343:         alu_op_b_mux_sel_o  = OP_B_IMM;
344:         imm_b_mux_sel_o     = IMMB_I;
345:         regfile_we          = 1'b1;
346: 
347:         unique case (instr_rdata_i[14:12])
348:           3'b000: alu_operator_o = ALU_ADD;  // Add Immediate
349:           3'b010: alu_operator_o = ALU_SLTS; // Set to one if Lower Than Immediate
350:           3'b011: alu_operator_o = ALU_SLTU; // Set to one if Lower Than Immediate Unsigned
351:           3'b100: alu_operator_o = ALU_XOR;  // Exclusive Or with Immediate
352:           3'b110: alu_operator_o = ALU_OR;   // Or with Immediate
353:           3'b111: alu_operator_o = ALU_AND;  // And with Immediate
354: 
355:           3'b001: begin
356:             alu_operator_o = ALU_SLL;  // Shift Left Logical by Immediate
357:             if (instr_rdata_i[31:25] != 7'b0)
358:               illegal_insn_o = 1'b1;
359:           end
360: 
361:           3'b101: begin
362:             if (instr_rdata_i[31:25] == 7'b0)
363:               alu_operator_o = ALU_SRL;  // Shift Right Logical by Immediate
364:             else if (instr_rdata_i[31:25] == 7'b010_0000)
365:               alu_operator_o = ALU_SRA;  // Shift Right Arithmetically by Immediate
366:             else
367:               illegal_insn_o = 1'b1;
368:           end
369: 
370:           default: illegal_insn_o = 1'b1;
371:         endcase
372:       end
373: 
374:       OPCODE_OP: begin  // Register-Register ALU operation
375:         regfile_we     = 1'b1;
376: 
377:         if (instr_rdata_i[31]) begin
378:           illegal_insn_o = 1'b1;
379:         end
380:         else
381:         begin // non bit-manipulation instructions
382: 
383:           if (~instr_rdata_i[28])
384: 
385:           unique case ({instr_rdata_i[30:25], instr_rdata_i[14:12]})
386:             // RV32I ALU operations
387:             {6'b00_0000, 3'b000}: alu_operator_o = ALU_ADD;   // Add
388:             {6'b10_0000, 3'b000}: alu_operator_o = ALU_SUB;   // Sub
389:             {6'b00_0000, 3'b010}: alu_operator_o = ALU_SLTS;  // Set Lower Than
390:             {6'b00_0000, 3'b011}: alu_operator_o = ALU_SLTU;  // Set Lower Than Unsigned
391:             {6'b00_0000, 3'b100}: alu_operator_o = ALU_XOR;   // Xor
392:             {6'b00_0000, 3'b110}: alu_operator_o = ALU_OR;    // Or
393:             {6'b00_0000, 3'b111}: alu_operator_o = ALU_AND;   // And
394:             {6'b00_0000, 3'b001}: alu_operator_o = ALU_SLL;   // Shift Left Logical
395:             {6'b00_0000, 3'b101}: alu_operator_o = ALU_SRL;   // Shift Right Logical
396:             {6'b10_0000, 3'b101}: alu_operator_o = ALU_SRA;   // Shift Right Arithmetic
397: 
398:             // supported RV32M instructions
399:             {6'b00_0001, 3'b000}: begin // mul
400:                 alu_operator_o        = ALU_ADD;
401:                 multdiv_operator_o    = MD_OP_MULL;
402:                 mult_int_en           = 1'b1;
403:                 multdiv_signed_mode_o = 2'b00;
404:                 illegal_insn_o        = RV32M ? 1'b0 : 1'b1;
405:             end
406:             {6'b00_0001, 3'b001}: begin // mulh
407:                 alu_operator_o        = ALU_ADD;
408:                 multdiv_operator_o    = MD_OP_MULH;
409:                 mult_int_en           = 1'b1;
410:                 multdiv_signed_mode_o = 2'b11;
411:                 illegal_insn_o        = RV32M ? 1'b0 : 1'b1;
412:             end
413:             {6'b00_0001, 3'b010}: begin // mulhsu
414:                 alu_operator_o        = ALU_ADD;
415:                 multdiv_operator_o    = MD_OP_MULH;
416:                 mult_int_en           = 1'b1;
417:                 multdiv_signed_mode_o = 2'b01;
418:                 illegal_insn_o        = RV32M ? 1'b0 : 1'b1;
419:             end
420:             {6'b00_0001, 3'b011}: begin // mulhu
421:                 alu_operator_o        = ALU_ADD;
422:                 multdiv_operator_o
[TRUNCATED]

11: ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
12: // Engineer:       Igor Loi - igor.loi@unibo.it                               //
13: //                                                                            //
14: // Additional contributions by:                                               //
15: //                 Andreas Traber - atraber@iis.ee.ethz.ch                    //
16: //                 Markus Wegmann - markus.wegmann@technokrat.ch              //
17: //                 Davide Schiavone - pschiavo@iis.ee.ethz.ch                 //
18: //                                                                            //
19: // Design Name:    Load Store Unit                                            //
20: // Project Name:   zero-riscy                                                 //
21: // Language:       SystemVerilog                                              //
22: //                                                                            //
23: // Description:    Load Store Unit, used to eliminate multiple access during  //
24: //                 processor stalls, and to align bytes and halfwords         //
25: //                                                                            //
26: ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
27: 
28: `include "zeroriscy_config.sv"
29: 
30: module zeroriscy_load_store_unit
31: (
32:     input  logic         clk,
33:     input  logic         rst_n,
34: 
35:     // output to data memory
36:     output logic         data_req_o,
37:     input  logic         data_gnt_i,
38:     input  logic         data_rvalid_i,
39:     input  logic         data_err_i,
40: 
41:     output logic [31:0]  data_addr_o,
42:     output logic         data_we_o,
43:     output logic [3:0]   data_be_o,
44:     output logic [31:0]  data_wdata_o,
45:     input  logic [31:0]  data_rdata_i,
46: 
47:     // signals from ex stage
48:     input  logic         data_we_ex_i,         // write enable                      -> from ex stage
49:     input  logic [1:0]   data_type_ex_i,       // Data type word, halfword, byte    -> from ex stage
50:     input  logic [31:0]  data_wdata_ex_i,      // data to write to memory           -> from ex stage
51:     input  logic [1:0]   data_reg_offset_ex_i, // offset inside register for stores -> from ex stage
52:     input  logic         data_sign_ext_ex_i,   // sign extension                    -> from ex stage
53: 
54:     output logic [31:0]  data_rdata_ex_o,      // requested data                    -> to ex stage
55:     input  logic         data_req_ex_i,        // data request                      -> from ex stage
56: 
57:     input  logic [31:0]  adder_result_ex_i,
58: 
59:     output logic         data_misaligned_o,    // misaligned access was detected    -> to controller
60:     output logic [31:0]  misaligned_addr_o,
61: 
62:     // exception signals
63:     output logic         load_err_o,
64:     output logic         store_err_o,
65: 
66:     // stall signal
67:     output logic         lsu_update_addr_o, // LSU ready for new data in EX stage
68:     output logic   
[TRUNCATED]

770: 
771: interface BIST_BUS;
772: 
773:    logic         bist_en;
774:    logic         bist_done_even;
775:    logic         bist_done_odd;
776:    logic         bist_result_even;
777:    logic         bist_result_odd;
778:    logic [31:0]  bist_pattern;
779: 
780:    // Master Side
781:    //***************************************
782:    modport Master
783:      (
784:       output     bist_en,
785:       input      bist_done_even,
786:       input      bist_done_odd,
787:       input      bist_result_even,
788:       input      bist_result_odd,
789:       output     bist_pattern
790:       );
791: 
792:    // Slave Side
793:    //***************************************
794:    modport Slave
795:      (
796:       input      bist_en,
797:       output     bist_done_even,
798:       output     bist_done_odd,
799:       output     bist_result_even,
800:       output     bist_result_odd,
801:       input      bist_pattern
802:       );
803: 
804: endinterface // BIST_BUS
805: 
806:    //********************************************************
807:    //****************** BRAM PORT BUS ***********************
808:    //********************************************************
809: 
810: interface BRAM_PORT;
811: 
812:    logic clk;
813:    logic rst;
814:    logic en;
815:    logic [ 7:0] we;
816:    logic [14:0] addr;
817:    logic [63:0] din;
818:    logic [63:0] dout;
819: 
820:    modport Master (
821:       output clk,
822:       output rst,
823:       output en,
824:       output we,
825:       output addr,
826:       output din,
827:       input  dout
828:    );
829: 
830:    modport Slave (
831:       input  clk,
832:       input  rst,
833:       input  en,
834:       input  we,
835:       input  addr,
836:       input  din,
837:       output dout
838:    );
839: 
840: endinterface // BRAM_PORT
841: 
842: 
843: interface SP_ICACHE_CTRL_UNIT_BUS;
844: 
845:     // ICACHE_CTRL UNIT INTERFACE
846:     //***************************************
847:     logic                 ctrl_req_enable;
848:     logic                 ctrl_ack_enable;
849:     logic                 ctrl_req_disable;
850:     logic                 ctrl_ack_disable;
851:     logic                 ctrl_pending_trans;
852:     logic                 flush_req;
853:     logic                 flush_ack;
854:     logic                 icache_is_private;
855: `ifdef FEATURE_ICACHE_STAT
856:     logic [31:0]          ctrl_hit_count;
857:     logic [31:0]          ctrl_trans_count;
858:     logic [31:0]          ctrl_miss_count;
859: 
860:     logic                 ctrl_clear_regs;
861:     logic                 ctrl_enable_regs;
862: 
863: `endif
864: 
865:     // Master Side
866:     //***************************************
867:     modport Master
868:     (
869:         output ctrl_req_enable,
870:         output ctrl_req_disable,
871:         output flush_req,
872:         output icache_is_private,
873:         input  flush_ack,
874: 
875:         input  ctrl_ack_enable,
876:         input  ctrl_ack_disable,
877:         input  ctrl_pending_trans
878:     `ifdef FEATURE_ICACHE_STAT
879:         ,
880:         input  ctrl_hit_count,
881:         input  ctrl_trans_count,
882:         input  ctrl_miss_count,
883:         output ctrl_clear_regs,
884:         output ctrl_enable_regs
885:     `endif
886:     );
887: 
888:     // Slave Side
889:     //***************************************

66: 
67:   // LSU

465:       ////////////////////////////////////////////////
466:       //  ____  ____  _____ ____ ___    _    _      //
467:       // / ___||  _ \| ____/ ___|_ _|  / \  | |     //

473: 
474:       OPCODE_SYSTEM: begin
475:         if (instr_rdata_i[14:12] == 3'b000)
476:         begin
477:           // non CSR related SYSTEM instructions
478:           unique case (instr_rdata_i[31:20])
479:             12'h000:  // ECALL
480:             begin
481:               // environment (system) call
482:               ecall_insn_o = 1'b1;
483:             end
484: 
485:             12'h001:  // ebreak
486:             begin
487:               // debugger trap
488:               ebrk_insn_o = 1'b1;
489:             end
490: 
491:             12'h302:  // mret
492:             begin
493:               mret_insn_o = 1'b1;
494:             end
495: 
496:             12'h105:  // wfi
497:             begin
498:               // flush pipeline
499:               pipe_flush_o = 1'b1;
500:             end
501: 
502:             default:
503:             begin
504:               illegal_insn_o = 1'b1;
505:             end
506:           endcase
507:         end
508:         else
509:         begin
510:           // instruction to read/modify CSR
511:           csr_access_o        = 1'b1;
512:           regfile_we          = 1'b1;
513:           alu_op_b_mux_sel_o  = OP_B_IMM;
514:           imm_a_mux_sel_o     = IMMA_Z;
515:           imm_b_mux_sel_o     = IMMB_I;    // CSR address is encoded in I imm
516: 
517:           if (instr_rdata_i[14] == 1'b1) begin
518:             // rs1 field is used as immediate
519:             alu_op_a_mux_sel_o = OP_A_IMM;
520:           end else begin
521:             alu_op_a_mux_sel_o = OP_A_REGA_OR_FWD;
522:           end
523: 
524:           unique case (instr_rdata_i[13:12])
525:             2'b01:   csr_op   = CSR_OP_WRITE;
526:             2'b10:   csr_op   = CSR_OP_SET;
527:             2'b11:   csr_op   = CSR_OP_CLEAR;
528:             default: csr_illegal = 1'b1;
529:           endcase
530: 
531:           if(~csr_illegal)
532:             if (instr_rdata_i[31:20] == 12'h300)
533:               //access to mstatus
534:               csr_status_o = 1'b1;
535: 
536:           illegal_insn_o = csr_illegal;
537: 
538:         end
539: 
540:       end
541:       default: begin
542:         illegal_insn_o = 1'b1;
543:       end
544:     endcase
545: 
546:     // make sure invalid compressed instruction causes an exception
547:     if (illegal_c_insn_i) begin
548:       illegal_insn_o = 1'b1;
549:     end
550: 
551:     // misaligned access was detected by the LSU
552:     // TODO: this section should eventually be moved out of the decoder
553:     if (data_misaligned_i == 1'b1)
554:     begin
555:       // only part of the pipeline is unstalled, mak

`define NB_SLAVE  4
`define NB_MASTER 4
`define NB_REGION 4

// MASTER PORT TO CLUSTER(3MB)
`define CLUSTER_DATA_START_ADDR  32'h1000_0000
`define CLUSTER_DATA_END_ADDR    32'h103F_FFFF

// MASTER PORT TO L2
`define SOC_L2_START_ADDR        32'h1C00_0000
`define SOC_L2_END_ADDR          32'h1FFF_FFFF

// MASTER PORT TO L2
`define SOC_L2_PRI_CH0_START_ADDR     32'h1C00_0000
`define SOC_L2_PRI_CH0_END_ADDR       32'h1C00_8000

`define SOC_L2_PRI_CH1_START_ADDR     32'h1C00_8000
`define SOC_L2_PRI_CH1_END_ADDR       32'h1C01_0000

`define SOC_L2_PRI_CH0_SCM_START_ADDR 32'h1C00_6000
`define SOC_L2_PRI_CH0_SCM_END_ADDR   32'h1C00_8000

`define ALIAS_SOC_L2_PRI_CH0_SCM_START_ADDR 32'h0000_6000
`define ALIAS_SOC_L2_PRI_CH0_SCM_END_ADDR   32'h0000_8000


// MASTER PORT TO SOC APB

// REGION TO SOC APB PERIPHERALS
`define SOC_APB_START_ADDR       32'h1A10_0000
`define SOC_APB_END_ADDR         32'h1A10_FFFF

// REGION TO FABRIC CONTROLLER PERIPHERAL INTERCONNECT PERIPHERALS
`define FC_PERIPH_APB_START_ADDR 32'h1B20_0000
`define FC_PERIPH_APB_END_ADDR   32'h1B20_3FFF

// REGION TO FABRIC CONTROLLER APB PERIPHERALS
`define FC_APB_START_ADDR        32'h1B30_0000
`define FC_APB_END_ADDR          32'h1B3F_FFFF

// MASTER PORT TO STDOUT(64KB)
`define SOC_STDOUT_START_ADDR    32'h1A11_0000
`define SOC_STDOUT_END_ADDR      32'h1A11_FFFF

244:   // if we are sleeping on a barrier let's just wait on the instruction

246:   assign core_busy_int = if_busy | ctrl_busy | lsu_busy;

248:   always_ff @(posedge clk_i, negedge rst_ni)
249:   begin
250:     if (rst_ni == 1'b0) begin
251:       core_busy_q <= 1'b0;
252:     end else begin
253:       core_busy_q <= core_busy_int;
254:     end
255:   end
256: 
257:   assign core_busy   = core_ctrl_firstfetch ? 1'b1 : core_busy_q;
258: 
259:   assign dbg_busy    = dbg_req | dbg_csr_req | dbg_jump_req | dbg_reg_wreq | debug_req_i;
260: 
261:   assign clock_en    = core_busy | dbg_busy | irq_i;
262: 
263:   assign sleeping    = (~core_busy);
